stemHtml: Select the correct statement regarding an auto-brake system during a rejected take-off.

options.a: Manual braking is always the best stopping technique for a rejected take-off.
options.b: In the rejected take-off mode, the auto-brake system can function as its own anti-skid system if the normal anti-skid system fails.
options.c: An auto-brake system disarms automatically when the aeroplane has come to a standstill after a rejected take-off.
options.d: When the rejected take-off mode is armed, the system will operate only when the take-off is aborted above a certain speed.
